children.label: i
children.text: information collected or maintained by or on behalf of the agency; and

children.label: ii
children.text: information systems used or operated by an agency or by a contractor of an agency or other organization on behalf of an agency;

children.label: i
children.text: information security standards promulgated under ;section 11331 of title 40

children.label: ii
children.text: operational directives developed by the Secretary under section 3553(b);

children.label: iii
children.text: policies and procedures issued by the Director;

children.label: iv
children.text: information security standards and guidelines for national security systems issued in accordance with law and as directed by the President;

children.label: v
children.text: emergency directives issued by the Secretary under section 3553(h); and

children.label: vi
children.text: responsibilities relating to assessing and avoiding, mitigating, transferring, or accepting supply chain risks under , and complying with exclusion and removal orders issued under section 1323 of such title; andsection 1326 of title 41

children.label: C
children.text: ensuring that information security management processes are integrated with agency strategic, operational, and budgetary planning processes;

children.label: A
children.text: assessing the risk and magnitude of the harm that could result from the unauthorized access, use, disclosure, disruption, modification, or destruction of such information or information systems;

children.label: B
children.text: determining the levels of information security appropriate to protect such information and information systems in accordance with standards promulgated under , for information security classifications and related requirements;section 11331 of title 40

children.label: C
children.text: implementing policies and procedures to cost-effectively reduce risks to an acceptable level; and

children.label: D
children.text: periodically testing and evaluating information security controls and techniques to ensure that they are effectively implemented;

children.label: i
children.text: carry out the Chief Information Officer’s responsibilities under this section;

children.label: ii
children.text: possess professional qualifications, including training and experience, required to administer the functions described under this section;

children.label: iii
children.text: have information security duties as that official’s primary duty; and

children.label: iv
children.text: head an office with the mission and resources to assist in ensuring agency compliance with this section;

children.label: B
children.text: developing and maintaining an agencywide information security program as required by subsection (b);

children.label: C
children.text: developing and maintaining information security policies, procedures, and control techniques to address all applicable requirements, including those issued under and ;section 3553 of this titlesection 11331 of title 40

children.label: D
children.text: training and overseeing personnel with significant responsibilities for information security with respect to such responsibilities; and

children.label: E
children.text: assisting senior agency officials concerning their responsibilities under paragraph (2);
ensure that the agency has trained personnel sufficient to assist the agency in complying with the requirements of this subchapter and related policies, procedures, standards, and guidelines;

ensure that the agency Chief Information Officer, in coordination with other senior agency officials, reports annually to the agency head on the effectiveness of the agency information security program, including progress of remedial actions;

ensure that senior agency officials, including chief information officers of component agencies or equivalent officials, carry out responsibilities under this subchapter as directed by the official delegated authority under paragraph (3); and
ensure that all personnel are held accountable for complying with the agency-wide information security program implemented under subsection (b).

periodic assessments of the risk and magnitude of the harm that could result from the unauthorized access, use, disclosure, disruption, modification, or destruction of information and information systems that support the operations and assets of the agency, which may include using automated tools consistent with standards and guidelines promulgated under ;section 11331 of title 40

children.label: A
children.text: are based on the risk assessments required by paragraph (1);

children.label: B
children.text: cost-effectively reduce information security risks to an acceptable level;

children.label: C
children.text: ensure that information security is addressed throughout the life cycle of each agency information system; and

children.label: i
children.text: the requirements of this subchapter;

children.label: ii
children.text: policies and procedures as may be prescribed by the Director, and information security standards promulgated under ;section 11331 of title 40

children.label: iii
children.text: minimally acceptable system configuration requirements, as determined by the agency; and

children.label: iv
children.text: any other applicable requirements, including standards and guidelines for national security systems issued in accordance with law and as directed by the President;
subordinate plans for providing adequate information security for networks, facilities, and systems or groups of information systems, as appropriate;

children.label: A
children.text: information security risks associated with their activities; and

children.label: B
children.text: their responsibilities in complying with agency policies and procedures designed to reduce these risks;

children.label: A
children.text: shall include testing of management, operational, and technical controls of every information system identified in the inventory required under section 3505(c);1So in original. Section 3505 contains two subsecs. (c).1

children.label: B
children.text: may include testing relied on in an evaluation under section 3555; and

children.label: C
children.text: shall include using automated tools, consistent with standards and guidelines promulgated under ;section 11331 of title 40
a process for planning, implementing, evaluating, and documenting remedial action to address any deficiencies in the information security policies, procedures, and practices of the agency;

children.label: A
children.text: shall be consistent with the standards and guidelines described in section 3556(b);

children.label: B

children.label: i
children.text: mitigating risks associated with such incidents before substantial damage is done;

children.label: ii
children.text: notifying and consulting with the Federal information security incident center established in section 3556; and

children.label: I
children.text: law enforcement agencies and relevant Offices of Inspector General and Offices of General Counsel;

children.label: II
children.text: an office designated by the President for any incident involving a national security system;

children.label: aa
children.text: not later than 7 days after the date on which there is a reasonable basis to conclude that the major incident has occurred; and

children.label: bb
children.text: after the initial notification under item (aa), within a reasonable period of time after additional information relating to the incident is discovered, including the summary required under subsection (c)(1)(A)(i); and

children.label: IV
children.text: any other agency or office, in accordance with law or as directed by the President; and
plans and procedures to ensure continuity of operations for information systems that support the operations and assets of the agency.

children.label: I
children.text: the threats and threat actors, vulnerabilities, and impacts relating to the incident;

children.label: II
children.text: the risk assessments conducted under section 3554(a)(2)(A) of the affected information systems before the date on which the incident occurred;

children.label: III
children.text: the status of compliance of the affected information systems with applicable security requirements at the time of the incident; and

children.label: IV
children.text: the detection, response, and remediation actions;

children.label: ii
children.text: the total number of information security incidents, including a description of incidents resulting in significant compromise of information security, system impact levels, types of incident, and locations of affected systems;

children.label: I
children.text: the number of individuals whose information was affected by the major information security incident; and

children.label: II
children.text: a description of the information that was breached or exposed; and

children.label: iv
children.text: any other information as the Director or the Secretary, in consultation with the Director, may require.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Each report submitted under subparagraph (A) shall be in unclassified form, but may include a classified annex.

children.heading: .—Access to information
children.label: ii .—Access to information
children.text: The head of an agency shall ensure that, to the greatest extent practicable, information is included in the unclassified version of the reports submitted by the agency under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: .—Other plans and reports
children.label: 2 .—Other plans and reports
children.text: Each agency shall address the adequacy and effectiveness of information security policies, procedures, and practices in management plans and reports.

children.label: A
children.text: the time periods; and

children.label: B
children.text: the resources, including budget, staffing, and training,
The description under paragraph (1) shall be based on the risk assessments required under subsection (b)(1).

children.heading: .—Public Notice and Comment
children.label: e .—Public Notice and Comment
children.text: Each agency shall provide the public with timely notice and opportunities for comment on proposed information security policies and procedures to the extent that such policies and procedures affect communication with the public.
